description: Chapter I discusses the various types of principalities
Chapter II covers hereditary principalities
Chapter III discusses difficulties of mixed principalities
Chapter IV explains why Darius's kingdom did not rebel
Chapter V covers governing cities that lived under their own laws

query: princes who gain power by their own ability
description: Chapter VI discusses new principalities acquired by one's own arms

query: princes who gain power through fortune
description: Chapter VII covers principalities acquired by fortune or ability

query: wickedness and criminal means to power
description: Chapter VIII discusses those who obtained power through wickedness
Chapter IX covers civil principalities arising from the people

query: strength and resources of a principality
description: Chapter X discusses how to measure a principality's strength
Chapter XI covers ecclesiastical principalities
Chapter XII discusses the problems with mercenary armies
Chapter XIII covers auxiliaries, mixed soldiery, and one's own

query: war as the prince's only profession
description: Chapter XIV discusses the art of war
Chapter XIX covers avoiding being despised and hated
Chapters XVII and XIX discuss fear vs love and avoiding hatred
Chapters VII and XXV discuss the role of fortune in politics
Chapters XII-XIV cover mercenary armies, auxiliaries, and war
Chapters VII and XXIV discuss losing states through fortune or poor governance
Chapters VIII and XVII discuss wickedness and cruelty vs clemency
Chapters XXII and XXIII cover secretaries and flatterers
Chapters XII and XXIV discuss mercenaries and Italian princes losing states
